title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the net force, we need to use the work-energy principle. The work done by the net force is equal to the change in kinetic energy of the car.

title: Kinetic Energy Formula
text: The initial kinetic energy of the car can be calculated using the formula: $$KE=\frac{1}{2} m v^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and v is the velocity.

title: Convert Speed to $$\frac{m}{s}$$
text: The initial speed is given in $$\frac{km}{h}$$. Convert it to $$\frac{m}{s}$$ by multiplying by $$\frac{5}{18}$$.

title: Calculate Initial Kinetic Energy
text: Substitute the values into the kinetic energy formula: $$KE=1050\frac{1}{2} \left(\frac{40\times5}{18}\right)^2$$.

title: Work Done by the Net Force
text: The work done by the net force is equal to the initial kinetic energy, which is also equal to the force times the stopping distance: $$Work=Force x$$.

title: Calculate the Net Force
text: Rearrange the work formula to find the force: $$Force=\frac{Work}{x}$$. Use the initial kinetic energy as the work done.
